title: Empirical case study — LangGraph (hand-built) vs CrewHaus (compiled)

metric1_boilerplateLoc.note: CrewHaus specs are the ones authored for THIS study (crewhaus-graph/, crewhaus-rag/), mirroring the hand-built baselines node-for-node. Bundles are real, deterministic compiler output.

upstreamStartersReference.note: starters/graph is a 3-node graph (not this study's 4-node baseline)

metric2_coldStart.method: isolated BUN_INSTALL_CACHE_DIR (user global cache never mutated); install/compile/first-ready reported as median of N runs to exclude one-time process/JIT warmup

crewhaus.note: no separate orchestration-install phase — runtime-core is one already-resolved published dependency

metric2_coldStart.interpretation: Warm cache: both sub-second and comparable. Cold cache is the deployment differentiator — hand-built must fetch+link a 34-package orchestration tree (~4.6s), CrewHaus is codegen over one resolved runtime dep (~0.41s): roughly an order of magnitude faster cold.

metric3_checkpointFailureCost.scenario: agent loops 15x accumulating context; step 16 network timeout drops the in-process MemorySaver; hand-built must re-send the lost window, CrewHaus re-sends only the durable pointer

measuredWindowApprox.note: the real 15-step window is model/run-dependent (observed offline 1708-1764, live 1731); deliberately not pinned to one value and NOT used for the headline, which sizes the lost window to the fixed 128k long-context regime

metric3_checkpointFailureCost.ratioNote: ratio = window/pointer = 128000/40 = 3200, independent of per-token price

durabilityEvidence.behaviour: 15 items accumulate, a checkpoint is written on every node transition, a FRESH store (new process) reads the durable head and resumes to completion

liveRun.source: metric-liverun.ts --json against api.anthropic.com; latency and token usage taken directly off the API response, cost via @crewhaus/cost-tracker computeCostMicros() (no hand-rolled arithmetic)

statefulGraphEndToEnd.trace: plan -> work -> reflect -> work -> reflect -> finalize
statefulGraphEndToEnd.note: latencyMs is the full 5-step end-to-end invoke; the token/cost figure is one marginal accumulating step (the cost SCALING under checkpoint failure is metric 3, computed deterministically)

liveRun.note: Live figures confirm both compiled-equivalent workloads execute end-to-end against a real model and return real latency + spend. This is the happy-path baseline; the headline cost-per-successful-run-under-failure result is metric 3.
